questions.question: Explain the four phases of the instruction cycle. How does each phase contribute to program execution?
keyPoints: FETCH: CPU obtains instruction from memory using address in PC
DECODE: CPU interprets the instruction to determine operation and operands
EXECUTE: ALU performs the operation or data is transferred
STORE: Results are written back to memory or register

questions.question: Describe the memory hierarchy in a computer system. Why is it designed in a hierarchical manner?
keyPoints: Levels: Registers, Cache, Main Memory, Secondary Storage
Speed decreases and capacity increases at each level
Cost per byte decreases at higher levels
Designed to balance speed, cost, and capacity needs
Uses principle of locality (spatial and temporal)

questions.question: What is dual-mode operation in operating systems? Explain why it is necessary for system security.
keyPoints: Two modes: User Mode and Kernel Mode (Privileged Mode)
User mode restricts direct hardware access
Kernel mode allows privileged operations
Prevents user programs from damaging system
Mode switch occurs via system calls and interrupts

questions.question: Differentiate between RISC and CISC architectures. Mention two advantages of each approach.
keyPoints: RISC: Simple instructions, fixed length, few addressing modes
CISC: Complex instructions, variable length, many addressing modes
RISC Advantages: Simpler design, faster instruction execution, pipelining easier
CISC Advantages: Fewer instructions per program, more powerful instructions, simpler compiler design

questions.question: Explain how interrupt-driven I/O works. How does it improve system efficiency compared to programmed I/O?
keyPoints: Devices send interrupt signal when ready
CPU saves current state and jumps to interrupt handler
Handler processes I/O and returns control to interrupted program
No CPU time wasted in continuous polling
CPU can perform other tasks while waiting for I/O

questions.question: What is the role of the Control Unit in a CPU? Explain how it coordinates instruction execution.
keyPoints: Generates control signals to direct processor operation
Controls data flow between ALU, registers, and memory
Manages timing of all operations using clock signals
Interprets instructions and determines required operations
Sequences through fetch-decode-execute cycle

questions.question: Explain the concept of DMA (Direct Memory Access). When is it particularly useful?
keyPoints: Allows I/O devices to transfer data directly to/from memory
CPU is freed from managing data transfer
DMA controller manages the transfer
Useful for large data transfers (disk, network, audio/video)
Reduces CPU overhead and improves overall system performance

questions.question: Explain the organization of the CPU in terms of its main components and their functions. How do these components interact during the execution of a machine instruction?
keyPoints: ALU: Performs arithmetic and logical operations
Control Unit: Generates control signals and coordinates operations
Registers: Fast storage locations for immediate data access
Buses: Connect components for data, address, and control signal transfer
Instruction cycle interaction: PC -> MAR -> Memory -> MDR -> IR -> Decoder -> ALU -> Result to register/memory

questions.question: Discuss the different types of buses in a computer system. Explain how data transfer occurs between the CPU, memory, and I/O devices.
keyPoints: Address Bus: Unidirectional, carries memory addresses
Data Bus: Bidirectional, carries actual data
Control Bus: Carries control signals (read, write, interrupt, clock)
System bus integrates all three
CPU places address on address bus, control signals on control bus
Data moves bidirectionally on data bus based on read/write signal

questions.question: What are system calls? Explain the mechanism of system calls and how they provide an interface between user programs and the operating system.
keyPoints: System calls are requests from user programs to kernel for privileged operations
Examples: file operations, process management, memory allocation
Mechanism: User program invokes system call via API, CPU switches to kernel mode
Parameters passed via registers or stack
Kernel executes the service and returns control to user program
Provides protection by restricting direct hardware access

questions.question: Describe the boot process of a computer system. Explain the role of the bootstrap loader and how the operating system is loaded into memory.
keyPoints: Power-on self test (POST) checks basic hardware
BIOS/UEFI initializes essential hardware components
Bootstrap loader in ROM is executed
Loader locates operating system kernel on boot device
Kernel is loaded into main memory
Kernel initializes system structures and starts system processes
Control passes to operating system for user login
